text.title: Trust Escalates, Then Auto-Revokes
text.summary: Elevated permission is bound to a specific incident, requires fresh MFA, and auto-revokes on incident close or timer expiry.

section.title: Escalation flow with MFA and auto-revoke
section.p: An on-caller types `/agent escalate-trust ` in the incident channel. The control plane (a) verifies the user is the listed on-caller for the service, (b) runs an MFA challenge, (c) creates the remediate RoleBinding scoped to the agent's ServiceAccount with a deadline annotation, and (d) schedules a revoke job. The binding disappears on the earliest of (incident closed | deadline reached).

rule.statement: Trust escalation MUST require an explicit `/agent escalate-trust <incident_id>` invocation by the verified on-caller, MUST require fresh MFA proof issued within the last 60 seconds, MUST create a remediate RoleBinding with TTL ≤ 60 minutes, and MUST auto-revoke on whichever fires first: incident-close webhook or TTL deadline. Escalations MUST NOT outlive their incident.
rule.rationale: The most common postmortem finding for agent-related incidents is "the elevation never expired". A self-revoking binding makes the leak structurally impossible; MFA binds the elevation to a specific human at a specific moment for the audit log.

example.code: def escalate_trust(incident_id, requesting_user): assert oncall.is_oncaller(incident_id, requesting_user) assert mfa.challenge_passed(requesting_user, max_age_s=60) binding = rbac.bind( sa="sre-agent", role="sre-agent-remediate", ttl_minutes=60, labels={"incident_id": incident_id, "approver": requesting_user}, ) schedule.revoke_at(binding, when=incident.closes_at(incident_id) or now()+60*60) audit.append(action="escalate", incident=incident_id, by=requesting_user)
example.note: On-caller check + MFA + TTL + revoke schedule, all in one transaction.

example.code: # Anti: long-lived elevation toggled by a flag agent.config.mode = "remediate" # forever, until someone remembers
example.note: No expiry, no MFA, no incident binding — every future incident inherits write privilege.

antipattern.reason: Trust is a property of the moment, not the service. The same agent during a calm afternoon and during a 3 a.m. confused incident are equally privileged — that is the leak.

section.reference: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit — time-boxed elevation.
Teleport — SOC2 patterns for ephemeral agent privilege.
